topic.what: Analysis of critical vulnerabilities (CVSS ≥9.0) identification and remediation timelines spanning 16 years
topic.how: Mixed-methods combining quantitative analysis of 245,456 CVE records (2009-2024) with qualitative case studies of Heartbleed, EternalBlue, Log4Shell
topic.why_matters: Critical vulnerabilities pose severe risks; understanding remediation delays helps organizations prioritize security investments and improve response efficiency

characteristics.what_it_measures: Average days from CVE reservation to public disclosure
characteristics.vs_baseline: Critical CVEs published faster than overall average in earlier years (57 vs 105 days in 2009)
characteristics.evidence: NVD and MITRE CVE database analysis
characteristics.context: CNA expansion post-2016 accelerated disclosure; convergence between critical and all CVEs in recent years

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time from disclosure to patch availability
characteristics.value: Mean 2,024 days, median 1,668 days for critical CVEs
characteristics.vs_baseline: Longer than all CVEs (mean 1,732 days, median 1,335 days)
characteristics.evidence: NVD modification timestamps as patch proxy
characteristics.context: 50% of critical CVEs patched within 1,668 days; 90% within 4,689 days; "last-mile" problem identified

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of CVEs classified as critical (CVSS ≥9.0)
Discrepancy between disclosure speed and remediation speed
characteristics.value: ~50x gap (33 days disclosure vs 1,668 days remediation)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Disclosure improved dramatically; remediation remained problematic

characteristics.what_it_measures: Variation in patch availability times across industries
characteristics.value: Range from <5 days (security vendors) to 2,000-4,000 days (commercial software)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Open source and cloud providers faster than traditional industries
characteristics.context: Healthcare, energy, telecommunications showed shorter cycles due to regulatory requirements

characteristics.value: Heartbleed: 6+ months avg remediation despite 2-day patch; EternalBlue: 20% unpatched after 6 months; Log4Shell: weeks for full remediation
characteristics.vs_baseline: Demonstrates gap between patch availability and organizational remediation
characteristics.evidence: Case study analysis of landmark vulnerabilities
characteristics.context: Illustrates organizational inertia beyond vendor response times

good_for: Priority setting for critical vulnerability remediation
requires: Historical CVE data spanning multiple years

novelty: Longitudinal benchmark for critical vulnerability remediation (2009-2024)
Sectoral comparison across 15+ industries with median analysis
Actionable recommendations grounded in mixed-methods evidence
open_problems: Right-censoring in recent years (2022+ data incomplete)
Sector classification relies on heuristic matching
Exploitation likelihood not measured for prioritization
Regulatory impact on patching requires further study
Automated remediation pipeline integration not addressed
